claim_boundary: The rotating rigid-rotor equilibrium solves the source-verified Rostoker & Qerushi (2002) one-dimensional one-ion centrifugal force balance d/dr[p + B_z^2/(2 mu_0)] = rho omega^2 r with the rigid-rotor density closure, reducing bit-exactly to the accepted Steinhauer no-rotation contract at theta_dot == 0. Verbatim Steinhauer 2011 Figure 3 digitised parity is NOT claimed and remains a separate external-parity gate; C-2U performance/topology references are context only, not a figure-parity certification.

python_status.claim_boundary: The rotating rigid-rotor equilibrium solves the source-verified Rostoker & Qerushi (2002) one-dimensional one-ion centrifugal force balance d/dr[p + B_z^2/(2 mu_0)] = rho omega^2 r with the rigid-rotor density closure, reducing bit-exactly to the accepted Steinhauer no-rotation contract at theta_dot == 0. Verbatim Steinhauer 2011 Figure 3 digitised parity is NOT claimed and remains a separate external-parity gate; C-2U performance/topology references are context only, not a figure-parity certification.
python_status.required_reference: Rostoker & Qerushi 2002 Phys. Plasmas 9 3057 (one space dimension, one ion); US 6,664,740 B2 rigid-rotor density closure
python_status.rotating_closure_reference: Rostoker & Qerushi 2002; US 6,664,740 B2 rigid-rotor density closure n_j = n_j(0) exp[(m_j omega_j^2 r^2/2 - e_j phi + e_j omega_j psi)/T_j]

rust_parity.required_reference: Rostoker & Qerushi 2002 Phys. Plasmas 9 3057 (one space dimension, one ion); US 6,664,740 B2 rigid-rotor density closure
rust_parity.rotating_closure_reference: Rostoker & Qerushi 2002; US 6,664,740 B2 rigid-rotor density closure n_j = n_j(0) exp[(m_j omega_j^2 r^2/2 - e_j phi + e_j omega_j psi)/T_j]

steinhauer_figure3_boundary.citation: L. C. Steinhauer, Review of field-reversed configurations, Physics of Plasmas 18, 070501 (2011).
steinhauer_figure3_boundary.notes: AIP publisher article and PDF endpoints returned HTTP 403 HTML payloads, not a PDF. The accepted no-rotation FRC implementation still needs verified Steinhauer Section II.B/Fig. 3 closure before rotating BVP promotion.
